subjectSystem.modelName: Global Bank plc — Retail Credit Risk Scoring Engine v4.2
subjectSystem.owner: Head of Retail Credit — Global Bank plc
subjectSystem.purpose: Probability-of-default scoring for unsecured consumer credit (credit cards, personal loans, overdraft) in 14 jurisdictions.
subjectSystem.trainingData: 14.2M historical accounts (2018-2025), 312 features (behavioural, bureau, open-banking), GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)+(f) + Art. 9(2)(b) lawful basis.
subjectSystem.architecture: Gradient-boosted trees (XGBoost 2.1) + monotonic calibration layer + shallow MLP explainer head; no LLM component; no generative capability.
subjectSystem.autonomyLevel: L2 (human-in-the-loop — decisions ≥£25k auto-referred to underwriter; <£25k auto-decisioned with free appeal channel).

riskTier.euAiAct: High-risk (Annex III §5(b) — creditworthiness assessment of natural persons)
riskTier.basel: IRB-adjacent; not regulatory IRB model but feeds provisioning & capital planning
riskTier.iso42001: In-scope AI system; critical; full AIMS lifecycle applies
riskTier.gdpr: Art. 22 ADM engaged; DPIA required; DPO signed-off 2026-02-14
riskTier.fcra_ecoa: Adverse action notices required; disparate-impact testing quarterly

subjectSystem.materiality: Top-5 material AI system in the group by RWA influence

regulatoryCoverage: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — Annex III §5(b), Annex IV full technical documentation, Art. 9 risk mgmt, Art. 10 data governance, Art. 12 logging, Art. 13 transparency, Art. 14 human oversight, Art. 15 accuracy/robustness/cybersec, Art. 17 QMS, Art. 43 conformity assessment, Art. 60 post-market monitoring, Art. 73 serious incident reporting
SR 11-7 (Fed/OCC) — Section III.A (development), III.B (implementation), III.C (use), IV (validation), V (governance)
Basel III (CRR/CRD) — Pillar 1 credit risk, Pillar 2 ICAAP, Pillar 3 disclosure; SA-CR and IRB adjacency
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Clauses 4-10 (context, leadership, planning, support, operation, performance, improvement); Annex A controls A.2-A.10
FCRA 15 U.S.C. §1681 — adverse action notices, reason codes, consumer dispute
ECOA 15 U.S.C. §1691 + Reg B — prohibited bases, disparate-treatment & disparate-impact
CFPB Circulars 2022-03 (adverse action) & 2023-03 (AI chatbots) — model explainability
FCA Consumer Duty (PRIN 2A) — foreseeable harm to retail customers
PRA SS1/23 — model risk management principles for banks
PRA SS2/21 — outsourcing & third-party risk (model vendors)
Global AI Governance & Compute Oversight Treaty Charter (GAGCOT) — GC1-GC7 crisis protocols
UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard (ATRS) — public-sector overlay where the bank serves HMG

executiveSummary: WP-032 operationalises a six-layer civilizational AI governance blueprint, grounded in a single, fully instantiated reference system: CRS-UUID-001, a Tier-1 retail credit-risk scoring engine at Global Bank plc. Every artefact is regulator-ready and cross-mapped to EU AI Act (including Annex IV), SR 11-7, Basel III/ICAAP, ISO/IEC 42001, GDPR, and FCRA/ECOA — with treaty-level overlays under the Global AI Governance & Compute Oversight Treaty Charter (GC1-GC7). The six layers span institutional MRM, sectoral supervision, frontier-compute attestation, geopolitical treaty alignment, an autonomous governance mesh (OPA/Rego + CI/CD + runtime), and adversarial co-evolution. Deliverables include a full Annex IV dossier, 12-tab audit pack, independent validation report, capital-impact assessment, AIMS evidence bundle, nine cryptographic evidence manifests (Merkle-anchored, post-quantum-ready), 12 OPA/Rego policies with 14 CI/CD gates, a supervisory replay kit, eight harmonized global supervisory reports, 13 multi-layer simulations (including GC1-GC7), and a Treaty Authority Implementation Charter (G-AGCOTA). The blueprint converts abstract frontier-AI governance into a board-defensible, prudential-supervisor-defensible, treaty-defensible operating reality.

L1_institutional.title: Institutional Governance — Bank-Internal Controls for CRS-UUID-001
L1_institutional.summary: Three-lines-of-defence MRM with SR 11-7 / SS1/23 alignment, ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS lifecycle, EU AI Act Annex IV technical documentation, and conduct controls under FCRA/ECOA/GDPR/Consumer Duty.

committees.name: Conduct & Customer Committee
committees.crsItem: Appeal rates, adverse-action disputes, Consumer Duty outcomes

crsArtefacts: Data sheet for CRS training corpus
Fairness report (4/5 rule, demographic parity, equalised odds)

annexIvDossier.instrument: EU AI Act Annex IV — Technical Documentation

structure.crsContent: Model name/version, intended purpose (retail PoD scoring EEA + GB), provider & deployer identification, SW/HW stack, release history
Architecture (XGBoost 2.1 + monotonic calibrator + MLP explainer), 312-feature list with provenance, training methodology, optimisation
Human-oversight protocol, £25k auto-referral threshold, override logging, real-time drift monitoring, kill-switch procedure
ISO 23894 risk register (47 risks), treatment plans, residual-risk acceptance by CRO, link to SR 11-7 model-risk register
Training/validation/test datasets, 14-jurisdiction provenance, bias-detection report, GDPR Art. 10 disclosures, data-minimisation proof
Change log since v3.0 (2023), CAB records, impact assessments, re-validation outcomes
ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23053, ISO/IEC 23894, ISO/IEC 25059, SR 11-7 internal
Signed by authorised representative; conformity-assessment route Art. 43(2) internal control + notified body for post-market monitoring
Drift KPIs, fairness KPIs, complaint-trend triggers, serious-incident definition & reporting SLAs, annual re-assessment

annexIvDossier.sizing: ≈640 pages; auto-generated from live evidence store; Merkle-anchored per section.

sr117Mapping.crsControl: Conceptual soundness review; feature-engineering documentation; developer peer review
Code freeze + SAST/DAST + reproducibility attestation; data-pipeline integrity; UAT sign-off
Business-user training; override policy; outcome-analysis loop
IMV annual full revalidation + quarterly ongoing monitoring; effective challenge log; open-issue MRIA tracking
Model inventory entry (active), tier assignment review, MRC minutes, Board Risk reporting, independent audit

FCRA.adverseActionNotice: Automated generation within 30 days; top-4 SHAP reason codes translated to plain-English
FCRA.consumerDisputeFlow: Model-level and case-level dispute routes; SLA 30 days; quarterly dispute-outcome MI
FCRA.accuracyObligation: §1681e(b) — monthly bureau-data reconciliation; exception report to Board

ECOA_RegB.disparateImpactTesting: Quarterly 4/5 rule + logistic regression residual analysis by protected class proxy
ECOA_RegB.remediationProtocol: If 4/5 rule fails → 90-day remediation plan → MRC escalation → customer remediation if confirmed

safeguards: Right to human intervention (appeal within 30 days)
GDPR_Art22.lawfulBasis: Art. 6(1)(b) contract performance + Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest (balancing test documented)

consumerDuty_PRIN2A.foreseeableHarm: Monitored via: (i) appeal overturn rate, (ii) complaint-root-cause analysis, (iii) vulnerable-customer outcomes MI, (iv) affordability distress indicators

L2_systemic.title: Systemic Governance — Sectoral & National Supervisory Coordination
L2_systemic.summary: PRA / FCA / OCC / Fed / ECB coordination for Tier-1 AI models, ICAAP Pillar-2 capital impact, supervisory colleges, cross-border recognition, and sector-wide stress testing.

modelRiskPillar2Addon.2026PostWP032: £26m RWA add-on (−38% as assurance depth rose)
modelRiskPillar2Addon.driverOfReduction: IMV effective-challenge evidence + ISO 42001 certification + Annex IV dossier completeness

stressScenarios.crsModelSensitivity: Geographic sector drift; PSI up to 0.18

supervisoryCollege.name: CRS-UUID-001 Supervisory College (chartered under Basel supervisory-college protocol)

harmonizedSupervisoryReports.title: Art. 73 Serious Incident Report template (0 YTD)

supervisoryReplayKit.purpose: Reproduce any individual decision or aggregate statistic under supervisory examination
components: Deterministic training pipeline image (Docker digest sha256:…)
Inference replay engine (given input → byte-identical output)
supervisoryReplayKit.slaToReproduce: ≤4 hours for individual decision; ≤24 hours for quarterly aggregate; ≤72 hours for full back-test

L3_frontierCompute.title: Frontier Compute Governance — Training Compute Register & Custody
L3_frontierCompute.summary: Even though CRS-UUID-001 is not a frontier model by scale, the blueprint instantiates the frontier-compute governance controls that will apply to the bank's larger AI systems (FraudNet-L, MarketCopilot) and demonstrates cross-model integration under GAGCOT compute-oversight rules.

computeRegister.frontierThresholdPolicy: Any training run ≥1e25 FLOPs requires 90-day pre-notification to G-AGCOTA + PRA; the CRS run is 2.4e6× below threshold but still logged for systemic transparency.

patterns.crsImplementation: Feature-flag kill-switch in API gateway; tested monthly; last test 2026-04-05 PASS (47s)
Blue-green deployment; automated traffic shift; last drill 2026-03-22 PASS (11m)
HSM-based weight-release revocation; dual-control; last drill 2026-02-18 PASS (22m)

regulatorMandated: PRA / FCA / OCC / Fed / ECB (direct to CEO)

postKillProtocol: Return-to-service gate: MRC + CRO + CAIO unanimous

weightCustody.model: Dual-control HSM with n-of-m (3-of-5) key escrow across geographically separated custodians
weightCustody.rotation: Quarterly key rotation; annual full re-attestation
weightCustody.pqReady: Post-quantum signatures (Dilithium-5) layered over ECDSA during transition

gpuAttestations.mechanism: SEV-SNP / Intel TDX confidential-VM attestation + CUDA-runtime measured boot
gpuAttestations.verifier: Internal attestation service (ATLAS) cross-signed by cloud provider attestation APIs
gpuAttestations.frequency: Per training run + per 1000 inferences in confidential-inference mode
gpuAttestations.crsUsage: Training attested; inference runs on standard (non-confidential) infra as CRS does not process regulated frontier-sensitive data

L4_geopoliticalTreaty.title: Geopolitical Treaty Governance — GAGCOT & Global AI Crisis Simulations (GC1-GC7)
L4_geopoliticalTreaty.summary: Treaty-level overlay for CRS-UUID-001 via GAGCOT (Global AI Governance & Compute Oversight Treaty Charter). CRS is a signatory-institution registered high-impact system subject to GC1-GC7 protocols.

implementationCharter.title: Treaty Authority Implementation Charter for G-AGCOTA

pillars.function: 24×7 Ops, intake, triage, escalation to crisis protocols
Ingest compute-register attestations; anomaly detection; quarterly public report
Merkle-DAG evidence receipts; supervisory replay service
Annual signatory review; publish compliance index
Publish evidence & report schemas; align ISO/NIST/ENISA mappings

implementationCharter.funding: Signatory assessed contributions (risk-weighted by supervisor AI market footprint)
implementationCharter.staffing: ≈220 FTE at steady state (Yr-3); secondments from national supervisors

gcScenarios.name: Cross-Border Capability Shock
gcScenarios.trigger: Emergence of >1e25 FLOPs model with disclosed autonomous-agent capability above evaluated safety envelope
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Indirect — triggers bank-wide AI capability reassessment; CRS itself unaffected

gcScenarios.name: Systemic Fairness Divergence
gcScenarios.trigger: ≥3 cross-border banks report coordinated 4/5-rule failures on retail credit AI systems within 30-day window
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Direct — CRS fairness metrics become sentinel indicators; harmonized supervisory college convened
actions: Cross-bank fairness-data sharing under treaty safe-harbour

gcScenarios.name: Compute-Supply Disruption
gcScenarios.trigger: Loss of >40% accessible training compute capacity in OECD due to geopolitical event
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Indirect — triggers fallback scorecard activation for CRS retraining

gcScenarios.name: Adversarial Data-Poisoning Campaign
gcScenarios.trigger: Detection of coordinated data-poisoning affecting ≥2 G-SIFI credit AI systems
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Direct — CRS is a canonical target; kill-switch rehearsal becomes live protocol

gcScenarios.name: Autonomous-Agent Containment Failure
gcScenarios.trigger: Exfiltration attempt or sandbox escape by an AI agent at any signatory institution
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Low (CRS is non-agentic) — but blueprint retains the protocol for consistency and agent-era readiness

gcScenarios.name: Model-Weight Compromise
gcScenarios.trigger: Confirmed exfiltration of weights for any treaty-registered model
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Direct — CRS weights are HSM-custody; breach triggers GC6 response
actions: Emergency rotation to v4.3 with re-trained weights

gcScenarios.name: Governance Dissolution Threat
gcScenarios.trigger: Signatory state withdrawal or coordinated attack on treaty infrastructure
gcScenarios.crsRelevance: Indirect — invokes governance continuity codex; CRS governance continues under national regulators
actions: Continuity of supervision via Ring-1 coalition
IMV re-runs back-test with immutable snapshot

L5_autonomousMesh.title: Autonomous Governance Mesh — Policy-as-Code, CI/CD & Cryptographic Evidence
L5_autonomousMesh.summary: OPA/Rego policies codify institutional, systemic, and treaty obligations, executing as deterministic gates across the CRS CI/CD pipeline and runtime. All decisions emit signed evidence artefacts that roll up into nine Merkle-anchored evidence bundles, producing a continuously-provable compliance posture.

meshArchitecture.controlPlane: OPA servers (Rego bundles) distributed across CI agents, K8s admission, API gateway, and feature-store
meshArchitecture.dataPlane: Signed decision logs → streaming pipeline → evidence-bundle writer → Merkle-DAG appender → WORM vault

opaPolicies.title: Annex IV completeness gate
opaPolicies.enforces: All 9 Annex IV sections present + Merkle-anchored

opaPolicies.title: 4/5 rule fairness gate
opaPolicies.enforces: No protected class below 0.80 disparate-impact ratio

opaPolicies.title: Adverse-action notice coverage
opaPolicies.enforces: 100% of declines have top-4 reason codes + notice generated

opaPolicies.title: Art. 22 safeguards
opaPolicies.enforces: Appeal route active + DPIA current + DPO sign-off ≤12m

opaPolicies.title: Evidence bundle integrity
opaPolicies.enforces: Signed Merkle root verified for EB-001..EB-009

opaPolicies.title: Compute-register entry
opaPolicies.enforces: Training run has register entry + attestation within 7d
opaPolicies.decision: alert; block subsequent runs after 30d grace

opaPolicies.title: Art. 73 incident SLA
opaPolicies.decision: escalate to CAIO on SLA breach

opaPolicies.title: Consumer Duty outcomes gate
opaPolicies.decision: trigger remediation plan; block promo pricing

ciCdGates.effect: Block commits from developer equal to designated IMV reviewer
Block on critical CVEs or incompatible OSS licenses
Verify evidence-bundle signatures match expected Merkle roots
Byte-identical build across two clean agents required
Block deploy if fairness tests fail any protected class
Block deploy if kill-switch tests stale
Block release if Annex IV sections not current
Block release if DPO sign-off expired
Block release if compute-register entry missing
Block release if GAGCOT attestation overdue
Runtime telemetry: alert if adverse-action notice coverage <100%
Consumer-duty KRI guard; auto-open corrective-action ticket

L6_adversarialCoEvo.title: Adversarial Co-Evolution — Red-Team, Threat Intel & Kill-Chain Playbooks
L6_adversarialCoEvo.summary: Continuous adversarial testing and threat-intel integration keep CRS-UUID-001 resilient against evolving attacks. Shared kill-chain taxonomy aligns with GAGCOT Art. 9 and peer institutions.

redTeamProgramme.cadence: Monthly targeted exercises + annual full-scope campaign + ad-hoc on threat-intel trigger
redTeamProgramme.independence: Mixed internal (GB-Offensive) + external vendor (BlueRed Consulting) rotating annually

killChainTaxonomy.aligned: GAGCOT Art. 9 shared kill-chain + MITRE ATLAS

phases.crsExample: Scrape public model-card details; open-banking API probe
Compromised OSS dependency; vendor data-feed manipulation
Poisoned feature in upstream bureau feed
Fairness-metric gaming (slow drift below detection threshold)
Systematic mis-scoring producing conduct harm or capital loss

threatIntelIntegration.correlationEngine: SIEM + OpenCTI + ATLAS tagger; automated case opening into GRC on high-confidence matches
threatIntelIntegration.sharingProtocol: TLP:AMBER for intra-college; TLP:GREEN+ for treaty-wide; TLP:RED only when active attack

outputs: Rego policy updates (e.g. new drift thresholds)

simulations.scenario: Simulate AUC drop to 0.71 and 4/5-rule breach simultaneously in UK+US+EU

simulations.name: Kill-Switch + Weight-Custody Drill
simulations.objective: End-to-end kill-switch under adversarial trigger with weight-custody revocation
simulations.scenario: GC4 simulated; invoke inference kill-switch; verify rollback and HSM freeze
simulations.passCriteria: Actuals within SLA; Rego P-006 confirms test freshness; post-kill protocol executed

simulations.name: GC4 Treaty Table-Top
simulations.objective: Multi-signatory coordinated response to poisoning campaign
simulations.scenario: Three banks report similar anomalies; GAGCOT emergency session convened
simulations.passCriteria: All signatories synchronised; public communique drafted; lessons logged to Art. 9 library

simulations.name: Policy-as-Code Chaos Test
simulations.objective: Resilience of OPA/Rego mesh under partial outage
simulations.scenario: Disable 30% of OPA replicas; attempt deploy violating P-002; observe fail-closed behaviour
kpis: Policy decision latency p99 <50ms in degraded state
simulations.passCriteria: Zero policy-bypass; evidence bundle EB-008 integrity preserved

simulations.name: Full-Scope Red-Team Campaign
simulations.scenario: 30-day campaign covering all 7 kill-chain phases with external + internal teams
simulations.passCriteria: Campaign report delivered; Rego policies refreshed; purple-team loop closed

simulations.name: GC1 Capability-Shock Rehearsal
simulations.scenario: Peer discloses >1e25 FLOPs model; emergency session

capitalImpactAssessment.title: Capital Impact Assessment — CRS-UUID-001 (Pillar-2 Model Risk)
capitalImpactAssessment.framework: Basel III · PRA SS1/23 · ICAAP Pillar-2

scenarioCapitalSensitivity.crsModelSensitivity: Geographic sector drift; PSI up to 0.18

capitalImpactAssessment.boardConclusion: The Board considers the Pillar-2 model-risk add-on of £26m adequate given current assurance depth (3.7/4.0) and residual uncertainty. The £85m management overlay provides additional buffer pending Annex IV notified-body review. Triggers for revision: (i) AUC <0.78 two consecutive quarters, (ii) 4/5 rule breach, (iii) Art. 73 serious incident, (iv) GAGCOT GC-series activation.

validationReport.authority: Group Head of IMV (reports to CRO)
conclusions: Conceptual soundness: ACCEPTABLE — architecture choice justified; monotonic constraints documented
Outcome analysis: ACCEPTABLE — out-of-time AUC 0.812, PSI 0.067, KS 0.48
Ongoing monitoring: ACCEPTABLE — KRI dashboard operational; drift monitoring 30-day rolling
Governance: ACCEPTABLE — MRC oversight, tier re-confirmed Tier-1
effectiveChallengeExamples: Challenged calibration layer → developer rebuilt with additional monotonic constraints (MRIA-042 closed)
Challenged fairness testing scope → added two proxy-class tests per CFPB guidance (MRIA-043 closed)
Challenged kill-switch evidence → monthly rehearsal cadence formalised (MRIA-044 in progress)
recommendations: Expand fairness proxies to include age-band interaction effects
Implement shadow-model divergence monitor with 3σ threshold

codeExamples.killSwitchProcedure:
# CRS-UUID-001 Kill-Switch Procedure (operator-facing)
# Target SLAs: runtime disable ≤60s · rollback ≤15m · HSM freeze ≤30m
# Invocation authority: CAIO or CISO or CRO (any 1 of 3) for emergency

set -euo pipefail

# 1. Runtime inference disable (≤60s)
kubectl -n crs-prod set env deploy/crs-inference CRS_DISABLED=true
aws apigateway update-stage --rest-api-id $CRS_API --stage-name prod \
  --patch-operations op=replace,path=/variables/CRS_DISABLED,value=true

# 2. Rollback to v4.1 (≤15m)
argocd app set crs-inference --revision v4.1.0 && argocd app sync crs-inference --prune

# 3. HSM weight-custody freeze (≤30m)
hsm-cli revoke --key-alias crs-uuid-001-weights --reason "emergency-kill-switch" \
  --approvers $CISO_KEY,$CTO_KEY --quorum 2-of-5

# 4. Evidence bundle freeze
evidence-cli freeze --bundles EB-001..EB-009 --reason "kill-switch-invoked" \
  --anchor-to merkle-dag

# 5. Notification fan-out
incident-cli fire --sev P1 \
  --notify pra,fca,occ,fed,ecb,ico,gagcot,board-risk,mrc \
  --art73-template crs-serious-incident.yaml

echo "Kill-switch invoked at $(date -u +%FT%TZ) — post-kill protocol engaged."
